finding.contract_claim: Implicit contract: "exploration" is produced in tests/test_triage.py and checked in scripts/triage_bootstrap.py with no shared definition
finding.observed_behavior: Extract shared constants to a common module so a rename triggers an import error instead of a silent mismatch
finding.triage_reasoning:
Reality: The bare string literal "exploration" appears in clearly related roles across at least four call sites with no shared constant or Literal type constraint:
• src/osoji/triage.py:211 — authoritative branch: `if mode == "exploration":` (mode typed as plain `str`)
• scripts/triage_bootstrap.py:277 — secondary check: `strip_evidence=(mode == "exploration")`
• scripts/triage_bootstrap.py:339 — CLI mapping: `("explore", "exploration")`
• tests/test_triage.py:203,230,243 — direct argument: `mode="exploration"`
No TriageMode constant, no Literal["claim","exploration","exploration-sdk"] annotation exists anywhere in the codebase.

Significance: This is an unnamed project obligation. A partial rename (e.g., triage.py updated but triage_bootstrap.py's secondary check on line 277 missed) silently flips `strip_evidence`, contaminating exploration-baseline traces without any runtime error or type-checker warning. The strip_evidence check is the most dangerous site because it is behaviorally distinct from just forwarding the mode string to decide_batch — it is a redundant re-interpretation of the mode concept in bootstrap logic.

Actionability: Concrete fix is to annotate the `mode` parameter with `Literal["claim", "exploration", "exploration-sdk"]` and/or extract module-level constants (`TRIAGE_MODE_CLAIM = "claim"`, `TRIAGE_MODE_EXPLORATION = "exploration"`) in triage.py, then import and use them in triage_bootstrap.py. This turns a silent mismatch into an import error or type-checker error.
finding.suggested_fix: In src/osoji/triage.py, change the mode parameter annotation from `str` to `Literal["claim", "exploration", "exploration-sdk"]` (importing Literal from typing). Optionally extract module-level string constants (e.g. `TRIAGE_MODE_EXPLORATION = "exploration"`) and have scripts/triage_bootstrap.py import and use them in the CLI mapping tuple and the strip_evidence comparison, so a rename propagates as an ImportError rather than a silent behavioral divergence.
